message:
chore(beads): close bd-ic6i.4 (BiDi Text Rendering) and bd-2ueu (Embedded Terminal Widget)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-k4lj.6","title":"Capability Simulator E2E Test Suite","description":"## Purpose
End-to-end validation of terminal capability simulation for testing.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Profile Accuracy (cap_profile_e2e.rs)
- xterm-256color capabilities
- xterm-16 color limitations
- vt100 basic cursor only
- dumb terminal minimal features

### 2. Capability Override (cap_override_e2e.rs)
- Runtime capability injection
- Thread-local isolation
- Override stacking
- Cleanup on scope exit

### 3. Degradation (cap_degrade_e2e.rs)
- Color fallback behavior
- Style attribute fallback
- Cursor movement fallback
- Mouse support fallback

### 4. Quirk Simulation (cap_quirk_e2e.rs)
- tmux nested cursor quirk
- Screen line wrap behavior
- Windows console limitations
- iTerm2 specific features

### 5. Integration (cap_integration_e2e.rs)
- Full app under different profiles
- Profile switching mid-test
- Cross-profile snapshots
- Performance comparison

## Logging Requirements
- INFO: Profile switches
- DEBUG: Capability queries and results
- TRACE: Quirk applications
- Metric: Render output differences

## Script Location
scripts/e2e/capability_sim/

## CI Matrix
- Run full suite under each profile
- Compare output across profiles
- Verify no capability leaks

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Capability Simulator E2E Test Suite
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-kkn1","title":"Implement Cross-Terminal Clipboard tests","description":"# Implement Cross-Terminal Clipboard tests

## Background
Part of Cross-Terminal Clipboard Abstraction (bd-2d2v). Tests ensure clipboard works across terminals.

## Unit Tests
- osc52_encoding_correct: OSC 52 sequence properly base64 encoded
- fallback_to_system_clipboard: When OSC 52 fails, use pbcopy/xclip/wl-copy
- read_clipboard_works: Can read back what was written
- large_content_handled: Multi-KB content works correctly
- binary_content_base64_encoded: Non-text content encoded properly

## Integration Tests
- copy_paste_roundtrip_in_iterm: Full cycle works in iTerm2
- copy_paste_roundtrip_in_wezterm: Full cycle works in WezTerm
- copy_paste_roundtrip_in_kitty: Full cycle works in Kitty
- fallback_works_on_dumb_terminal: System clipboard used when OSC 52 unavailable

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All unit tests pass
- [ ] Integration tests pass on major terminal emulators
- [ ] Graceful fallback when clipboard not available","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:33:38.691212422Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T20:46:03.313064402Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T20:46:03.312998518Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-kkn1","depends_on_id":"bd-2d2v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-l0ko","title":"Add tracing spans to all render phases","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:24:10.149920273Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T20:34:12.480899941Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T20:34:12.480879152Z","close_reason":"Added tracing spans to all render phases: render_frame (info), present_ui (info), present_inline/altscreen diff_compute+emit+clear_ui+scroll_region (debug), emit_diff (debug with run count)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l0ko","depends_on_id":"bd-em01","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-l8x9","title":"Visual FX Modularization: Composable Backdrops + Library Effects (Metaballs/Plasma) + Optional GPU","description":"Extract the demo-only visual effects (metaballs/plasma first) into `ftui-extras` as reusable, composable, high-performance backdrops and effect primitives; enable alpha-correct compositing behind real widgets; design an optional GPU backend with silent failover to CPU.

## Context
The showcase implements impressive FX directly inside:
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/visual_effects.rs`

Today those algorithms are demo-owned and mostly render via:
- `ftui_extras::canvas::{Painter, Canvas}`

That prevents reuse behind real widgets (markdown panels, dashboards) where we want:
- correct alpha compositing into `cell.bg`
- legibility/contrast knobs (scrim)
- deterministic, allocation-free rendering

## Success Criteria (User-Visible)
- `ftui-extras` exposes a feature-gated `visual_fx` module:
  - core types (`BackdropFx`, `FxQuality`, `ThemeInputs`)
  - `Backdrop` widget + composition helpers
  - extracted `MetaballsFx` + `PlasmaFx` (with presets + quality scaling)
- Backdrops can be composed behind real widgets with predictable results:
  - base fill (opaque) + effect opacity + optional scrim
  - markdown-over-backdrop demo integration (bd-l8x9.7)
- Demo showcase uses the extracted library code:
  - Phase 1: remove per-frame Painter allocations (bd-l8x9.5.1)
  - Phase 2: metaballs/plasma via BackdropFx (cell-space) (bd-l8x9.5.2)
  - Optional: regain hi-res via canvas adapters (bd-l8x9.6 + bd-l8x9.5.3)
- Tests are first-class:
  - unit tests for bg-only invariant, alpha correctness, determinism, tiny areas (bd-l8x9.8)
  - E2E scripts with detailed, actionable logs (bd-l8x9.8.1/8.2)
- Performance is defended:
  - benchmarks / budgets for backdrop+FX (bd-l8x9.9)
- Optional GPU acceleration (if enabled) is seamless:
  - uses GPU when available
  - **never** errors or panics; always silently falls back to CPU (bd-l8x9.11–14)

## Non-Goals (This Epic)
- Extracting every single showcase effect immediately.
- Forcing GPU requirements; CPU is the baseline.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:30:26.788253805Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T18:57:49.686184370Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","extras","performance","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9","depends_on_id":"bd-3vbf","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":499,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Context

The demo showcase has genuinely impressive full-screen visual effects (metaballs, plasma, fire, matrix, etc.) implemented directly inside:
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/visual_effects.rs`

Right now these effects are *demo-owned* algorithms. They render via:
- `ftui_extras::canvas::{Painter, Canvas}` (Braille/sub-cell rasterization)

This is great for a wow-factor demo, but it prevents reuse inside real UI components (markdown panels, dashboards, forms) where we want:
- alpha-correct compositing behind widgets
- predictable contrast/legibility guarantees
- zero-flicker and deterministic presentation
- insanely high performance (no per-frame allocations, cache-friendly loops)

## Goal

Turn “demo visual effects” into **library-grade, trivially composable primitives** in `ftui-extras`:
- Effects can render as a **cell-background backdrop** behind arbitrary widgets (markdown, panels, lists).
- Effects can optionally also render as **high-resolution canvas output** for full-screen screensaver-like demos.
- Composition must be correct: use alpha blending and preserve scrollback/inline mode invariants.

Initial extraction scope:
- Metaballs + Plasma (because they are visually strong and structurally simple).

## Why This Serves FrankenTUI’s Core Goals

FrankenTUI’s differentiators are correctness + determinism + composability. Background effects are a perfect stress-test:
- If the API makes backdrops easy to layer correctly, *everything* becomes easier to compose.
- If the rendering model enforces zero surprise (no terminal corruption; no implicit I/O), it aligns with the one-writer rule.

We already have key low-level primitives:
- `PackedRgba::over()` implements Porter-Duff SourceOver.
- `Buffer::push_opacity()` + background compositing in `Buffer::set()` enable layered blending.

## Architectural Direction (High-Level)

We introduce a “backdrop render target” alongside the existing canvas target:

1) Backdrop target (cell-space):
- effect computes a `w*h` array of `PackedRgba` backgrounds
- widget writes those colors into `frame.buffer` as background-only pixels

2) Canvas target (sub-cell space):
- effect computes sub-pixel intensity/color into a `Painter` (Braille/blocks)
- `Canvas` renders it as glyphs

The critical composability primitive is the **Backdrop widget** (background-only), because it composes under any widget without affecting glyphs.

## Proposed Public API Sketch (Subject To Iteration)

```rust
pub enum FxQuality {
    Full,
    Reduced,
    Minimal,
}

pub trait BackdropFx {
    /// Fill `out` (len = w*h) with background colors in cell-space.
    ///
    /// Design constraints:
    /// - No per-call allocations (caller owns buffers).
    /// - Deterministic output given inputs.
    /// - Must tolerate tiny areas.
    fn render_bg(
        &mut self,
        out: &mut [PackedRgba],
        w: u16,
        h: u16,
        t: f32,
        theme: &ThemeInputs,
        quality: FxQuality,
    );
}

pub struct Backdrop<E> { /* effect + opacity + base fill + scrim policy */ }

impl<E: BackdropFx> Widget for Backdrop<E> { /* writes only cell.bg */ }
```

Theme input is an explicit decision point (see tasks):
- likely accept either `ftui_style::ResolvedTheme` (preferred stable surface)
- or a small `ThemeInputs` struct derived from `ftui-extras::theme::ThemePalette`

## Legibility / Contrast Policy

Backdrops must be safe behind text.
We achieve this by default through:
- an explicit opaque base fill (theme bg)
- effect composited with configurable opacity (usually 0.15–0.40)
- optional scrim overlay (theme overlay) under text-heavy regions

This creates a reliable invariant: text widgets can remain “transparent bg” and still be readable.

## Performance Non-Negotiables

- Zero allocations on the render hot path.
- Cache-friendly scanning (row-major), minimize branching.
- Avoid per-frame `Painter::for_area()` allocations in showcase; reuse buffers.
- Map `Frame.buffer.degradation` -> `FxQuality` so heavy effects degrade gracefully.

## Optional GPU Backend (Feature-Gated)

If a GPU is available, it should be used *only* as an internal accelerator and must:
- never panic or error to the user
- silently fall back to CPU forever if init fails
- be deterministic enough for interactive use (snapshot tests should default to CPU)

Planned implementation shape:
- `fx-gpu` feature using `wgpu`
- `FxBackend::Auto` with cached “GPU available” decision via `OnceLock`
- GPU computes the same `PackedRgba` cell-space buffer; terminal emission remains CPU

## Deliverables

- `ftui-extras`: new visual-fx/backdrop module(s) with stable-ish API
- extracted `MetaballsFx` + `PlasmaFx` using the new API
- demo showcase refactor to use the extracted library code
- tests for blending correctness + determinism, and perf sanity checks

## Non-Goals (For This Epic)

- extracting every single demo effect immediately (metaballs/plasma first)
- requiring GPU (GPU is optional and must never break CPU-only builds)
- changing terminal output/presenter mechanics
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:30:57Z"}]}

- Forcing GPU requirements; CPU is the baseline.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:30:26.788253805Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T19:27:00.821685369Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T19:27:00.821667114Z","close_reason":"All 14 subtasks completed: API spec, backdrop widget, metaballs/plasma extraction, demo integration, canvas adapters, UI integration, blending tests, performance profiling, docs, GPU backend (spike, impl, fallback, heuristics)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","extras","performance","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9","depends_on_id":"bd-3vbf","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":499,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Context

- predictable contrast/legibility guarantees
- zero-flicker and deterministic presentation
- insanely high performance (no per-frame allocations, cache-friendly loops)

Turn “demo visual effects” into **library-grade, trivially composable primitives** in `ftui-extras`:

- Effects can optionally also render as **high-resolution canvas output** for full-screen screensaver-like demos.
- Composition must be correct: use alpha blending and preserve scrollback/inline mode invariants.

Initial extraction scope:

## Why This Serves FrankenTUI’s Core Goals

We already have key low-level primitives:

2) Canvas target (sub-cell space):
- effect computes sub-pixel intensity/color into a `Painter` (Braille/blocks)
- `Canvas` renders it as glyphs

pub enum FxQuality {

    Minimal,

pub trait BackdropFx {
    /// Fill `out` (len = w*h) with background colors in cell-space.
    ///
    /// Design constraints:
    /// - No per-call allocations (caller owns buffers).
    /// - Deterministic output given inputs.
    /// - Must tolerate tiny areas.
    fn render_bg(
        &mut self,
        out: &mut [PackedRgba],

        t: f32,
        theme: &ThemeInputs,
        quality: FxQuality,

}

pub struct Backdrop<E> { /* effect + opacity + base fill + scrim policy */ }

impl<E: BackdropFx> Widget for Backdrop<E> { /* writes only cell.bg */ }

- likely accept either `ftui_style::ResolvedTheme` (preferred stable surface)

If a GPU is available, it should be used *only* as an internal accelerator and must:

- silently fall back to CPU forever if init fails
- be deterministic enough for interactive use (snapshot tests should default to CPU)

Planned implementation shape:
- `fx-gpu` feature using `wgpu`
- `FxBackend::Auto` with cached “GPU available” decision via `OnceLock`
- GPU computes the same `PackedRgba` cell-space buffer; terminal emission remains CPU

 {"id":"bd-l8x9.1","title":"Visual FX API Spec: BackdropFx, Theme Inputs, Quality/Degradation","description":"## Purpose
Define the stable, library-grade contract for **cell-background visual FX backdrops** in `ftui-extras`.

This is the spec bead that all implementation beads (Backdrop widget, extracted effects, canvas adapters, GPU backend) must align to.

## Key Decisions (Lock These Down)

1) **Render target + ownership**
- Primary target is **cell-space backgrounds**.
- Effects write into a caller-owned `&mut [PackedRgba]` where `len == (w*h)`.
- No per-frame allocations required by the trait.

2) **Determinism contract**
- Output must be deterministic given the explicit inputs (dims, time, theme, params, quality).
- No hidden globals, no internal RNG unless explicitly seeded.

3) **Time semantics**
- Pass absolute time `t` (seconds, `f32` or `f64`) into render calls.
- If an effect needs internal state, it can keep it, but must still be deterministic under a fixed input sequence.

4) **Theme boundary**
- FX consumes a small resolved `ThemeInputs` (slots expressed in `PackedRgba`).
- Conversions from `ftui_style::ResolvedTheme` and `ftui_extras::theme::ThemePalette` are separate work (bd-l8x9.1.2).

5) **Quality / degradation**
- Define `FxQuality` and an explicit mapping from `ftui_render::budget::DegradationLevel`.
- Decorative FX must be able to go `Off` at low budgets.

6) **Composition semantics**
- Backdrop writes **background only** (`cell.bg`) and must never emit glyphs.
- Default composition should be predictable:
  - start from an **opaque base fill** (theme bg/surface)
  - composite effect color at configured opacity
  - optional scrim policy for legibility
  - child widget renders normally above

## Output Artifacts
- Spec comments + examples here.
- A short reference doc: `docs/concepts/visual-fx.md` (module path + invariants + examples).
- Downstream tasks reference this bead for naming + semantics (not re-deciding ad hoc).

## Non-Goals (This Epic)
- Extracting every showcase effect immediately (metaballs/plasma first).
- GPU bit-identical determinism across drivers (CPU remains the reference for tests).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A clear, minimal public API in `ftui-extras` for backdrop effects.
- [ ] A documented mapping from render budget degradation → FX quality.
- [ ] Theme integration boundary decided and documented (with conversions).
- [ ] No per-frame allocations required by the trait (caller-owned buffers).
- [ ] API supports deterministic output and tiny-area correctness.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Public type names + module paths are explicitly stated and consistent (aligned with bd-l8x9.1.1 + bd-l8x9.1.4).
- `BackdropFx` contract includes: buffer ownership, no-alloc expectation, determinism expectations, tiny-area safety.
- `ThemeInputs` required slots are listed and justified (what each is used for).
- `FxQuality` levels + `DegradationLevel -> FxQuality` mapping are explicit and documented.
- Composition ordering (base fill -> effect -> scrim -> child) is spelled out so users get predictable results.
- Spec includes a concrete test obligation list (unit tests + E2E script coverage) without requiring per-frame logging.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:31:04.497198917Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T00:19:12.829177724Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T00:19:12.829158298Z","close_reason":"Spec codified as ftui_extras::visual_fx + docs/concepts/visual-fx.md; follow-ups in bd-l8x9.1.2 and bd-l8x9.1.3","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","extras","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":500,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Problem

We need a library-grade API for visual effects that:
- can render behind arbitrary widgets (cell-background target)
- can optionally render at sub-cell resolution (canvas target)
- remains composable, deterministic, and fast

The API must minimize coupling and avoid pulling heavy deps unless feature-gated.

## Decisions To Lock Down

### 1) Theme Input Type

We currently have *two* theme-ish systems:
- `ftui-style::Theme` / `ResolvedTheme` (intended public surface)
- `ftui-extras::theme::ThemePalette` (lots of tokens used by demos)

We must choose an input type for FX that is:
- stable and ergonomic
- cheap to pass
- supports both dark/light and semantic slots

Options:
A) Accept `ftui_style::ResolvedTheme` (preferred long-term; stable surface)
B) Accept a small `ThemeInputs { base_bg, surface_bg, accent1, accent2, ... }` derived from either system
C) Accept raw `PackedRgba` params only (max decoupling, but less composable)

Recommendation: B) `ThemeInputs` as the FX boundary, with conversions from `ResolvedTheme` and from `ThemePalette`.
This avoids forcing FX to depend on the entire theme system while still enabling deep integration.

### 2) Backdrop FX Trait Shape

We need a single "cell-space" contract:
- caller owns reusable buffers
- effect fills `out` (len = w*h) with `PackedRgba` background colors
- effect may keep internal state (for animations / noise) but must not allocate per frame

Key constraint: effect must tolerate tiny areas and be deterministic given inputs.

### 3) Quality / Degradation Mapping

We already have runtime degradation levels (`ftui_render::budget::DegradationLevel`) that widgets use.
We need to map that to `FxQuality`:
- `Full` -> full resolution / full iterations
- reduced -> fewer iterations / lower frequency detail
- minimal -> very cheap fallback (e.g., simple gradient/noise)

We should explicitly state: effects may choose to render nothing at `EssentialOnly` / `Skeleton` if they are decorative.

### 4) Canvas Target (Optional)

The showcase uses `Painter` + `Canvas` (Braille). The library should support this without requiring it for backdrops.
We need to decide:
- separate trait `CanvasFx` OR
- adapter layer where an effect can implement both targets

Recommendation: keep backdrop API primary; add canvas rendering later as a separate trait to avoid bloating the core.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A clear, minimal public API in `ftui-extras` for backdrop effects.
- [ ] A documented mapping from render budget degradation -> FX quality.
- [ ] Theme integration boundary decided and documented (with conversions).
- [ ] No per-frame allocations required by the trait (caller-owned buffers).
- [ ] API supports deterministic output and tiny-area correctness.

## Output Artifact

This issue is "done" when its decisions are codified as:
- an API sketch in description/comments AND
- concrete type names + module path chosen for implementation tasks.

 {"id":"bd-l8x9.1.1","title":"API Spec Subtask: Module Layout + Cargo Feature Flags","description":"## Goal
Choose module paths and Cargo feature gates for visual FX so:
- CPU-only users pay ~0 cost unless they opt in
- GPU deps are entirely opt-in
- canvas adapters don’t force canvas on the backdrop API

## Proposed Module Layout

`crates/ftui-extras/src/visual_fx/`
- `mod.rs`
- `backdrop.rs` (Backdrop widget + scrim + composition helpers)
- `theme_inputs.rs` (ThemeInputs type; conversions live here or in a submodule)
- `quality.rs` (FxQuality + mapping from DegradationLevel)
- `effects/metaballs.rs`
- `effects/plasma.rs`
- `canvas/` (adapters; `cfg(feature = "canvas")`)
- `gpu/` (wgpu backend; `cfg(feature = "fx-gpu")`)

## Proposed Feature Flags

Keep this tight (avoid a feature explosion):
- `visual-fx`:
  - enables core types + Backdrop widget + CPU helpers
  - depends on `ftui-render` + `ftui-widgets`
- `visual-fx-metaballs` (depends on `visual-fx`)
- `visual-fx-plasma` (depends on `visual-fx`)
- `fx-gpu` (optional):
  - depends on `visual-fx-metaballs`
  - pulls `wgpu`

Canvas adapters should be gated by existing `canvas` feature:
- `cfg(all(feature = "canvas", feature = "visual-fx"))`

## Validation Plan
- Update `scripts/widget_api_e2e.sh` feature matrix (tracked in bd-l8x9.8.4).
- Add a quick `cargo tree` sanity note in docs: `wgpu` must only appear with `--features fx-gpu`.

## Tests / Validation
- Feature matrix checks in `scripts/widget_api_e2e.sh` (see bd-l8x9.8.4).
- CI `cargo check` for core-only, each feature, and all features together.
- Log enabled features and resulting dependency tree in JSONL for failures.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Feature graph keeps CPU-only builds lean.
- [ ] GPU deps are entirely behind `fx-gpu`.
- [ ] Module naming is consistent with existing `ftui-extras` structure.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Module paths are consistent across the epic and match what bd-l8x9.1.4 implements.
- `wgpu` is not pulled into default builds; it is only present when `fx-gpu` is enabled.
- Canvas adapters are not compiled unless `canvas` is enabled.
- CI has a compile-check matrix for the new FX features (bd-l8x9.8.4).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"codex","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:31:30.844024582Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T00:17:52.757217104Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T00:17:52.757194361Z","close_reason":"Implemented visual-fx feature flags + module export surface","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","extras"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":503,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.1.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Purpose

Keep FX composability high while keeping compile times and dependencies under control.

## Proposed Module Layout

- `crates/ftui-extras/src/visual_fx/mod.rs`
  - `backdrop.rs` (Backdrop widget + composition helpers)
  - `quality.rs` (FxQuality + mapping from DegradationLevel)
  - `theme_inputs.rs` (ThemeInputs + conversions)
  - `effects/metaballs.rs`
  - `effects/plasma.rs`
  - `gpu/` (feature-gated)

Alternative: a single `visual_fx.rs` file initially, then split when stable.

## Proposed Feature Flags

- `visual-fx` (CPU baseline)
  - depends on: `ftui-render` (PackedRgba, Buffer), `ftui-widgets` (Widget), optionally `ftui-style` (theme conversions)
- `fx-gpu` (optional accelerator)
  - depends on: `wgpu` (or alternative)
  - must compile out cleanly when disabled

Notes:
- Keep `canvas` separate. FX backdrops should not require Braille/canvas.
- Canvas demos can be adapters layered on top of the backdrop API later.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Feature graph keeps CPU-only builds lean.
- [ ] GPU deps are entirely behind `fx-gpu`.
- [ ] Module naming is consistent with existing `ftui-extras` structure.

 {"id":"bd-l8x9.1.2","title":"API Spec Subtask: Theme Boundary + Conversions (ResolvedTheme / ThemePalette)","description":"## Goal
Define the **theme boundary** for visual FX:
- FX consumes a small resolved `ThemeInputs` (slots as `PackedRgba`)
- conversions from existing theme systems are explicit and cheap
- no cyclic deps / no global theme lookup from FX code

## Scope

1) **ThemeInputs slot list** (what FX needs, and why)
- `bg_base` / `bg_surface`: opaque fills so Backdrop output is deterministic and doesn’t depend on previous cell.bg.
- `bg_overlay`: scrim color.
- `fg_primary` / `fg_muted`: used by contrast helpers.
- `accent_primary` / `accent_secondary` (+ a small `accent_slots` array): used for palettes/presets.

2) **Conversions**
- `impl From<ftui_extras::theme::ThemePalette> for ThemeInputs`
- `impl From<ftui_style::ResolvedTheme> for ThemeInputs`
  - requires a stable `ftui_style::Color -> PackedRgba` mapping.

3) **Caching guidance**
- Conversions should be cacheable at the app/screen level (e.g., recompute on theme change, not per cell).
- Backdrop render calls take `&ThemeInputs` to keep costs explicit.

## Non-Goals
- No new global theme registry.
- No "compatibility shims": once the semantic design system lands (bd-3vbf.1), update the mapping directly.

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests: conversions are deterministic and produce opaque backgrounds where required.
- Unit tests: missing/None theme slots fall back to sensible defaults (documented).
- Unit tests: `ThemeInputs` stays within byte bounds and never produces NaN/inf (trivial for u8 RGBA).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] FX code can be used with either theme system.
- [ ] No dependency cycle introduced.
- [ ] ThemeInputs is small and cheap to pass by reference.
- [ ] ThemeInputs is sufficient for Metaballs/Plasma without hardcoding demo palettes.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- ThemeInputs is the only theme boundary for FX modules (no global theme lookups).
- Conversions exist for both `ResolvedTheme` and `ThemePalette` without introducing crate cycles.
- Defaults/fallbacks are documented and tested.
- Works today and remains compatible with future design system improvements (bd-3vbf.1 is related, not blocking).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:31:45.976257349Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T00:24:21.957915906Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T00:24:21.957895648Z","close_reason":"Implemented ThemeInputs expansion with semantic slots and From conversions for ThemePalette and ResolvedTheme. All tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","extras","theme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3vbf.1","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":504,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.1.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Context

FX needs theme-aware colors (base background, overlay, accents) but should not hard-depend on any single theme implementation.

We have:
- `ftui_style::Theme` / `ResolvedTheme` using `ftui_style::Color`
- `ftui_extras::theme::ThemePalette` using `PackedRgba`

## Proposal: ThemeInputs (FX boundary)

Define a small struct in the FX module:

```rust
pub struct ThemeInputs {
    pub bg_base: PackedRgba,
    pub bg_surface: PackedRgba,
    pub bg_overlay: PackedRgba,
    pub fg_primary: PackedRgba,
    pub fg_muted: PackedRgba,
    pub accent_primary: PackedRgba,
    pub accent_secondary: PackedRgba,
    pub accent_slots: [PackedRgba; 4], // keep small; effects can derive more
}
```

Then provide conversions:
- `impl From<ftui_extras::theme::ThemePalette> for ThemeInputs`
- `impl From<ftui_style::ResolvedTheme> for ThemeInputs`
  - requires a tiny `Color -> PackedRgba` conversion (likely via `ftui_style` -> `ftui_render` mapping).

## Dependency / Cycle Considerations

- FX lives in `ftui-extras`, which already has optional deps on `ftui-style` and `ftui-render`.
- Keep conversions feature-gated (e.g., `visual-fx` enables `ftui-render`; theme conversions can be behind `dep:ftui-style` if needed).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] FX code can be used with either theme system.
- [ ] No dependency cycle introduced.
- [ ] ThemeInputs is small and cheap to pass by reference.
- [ ] ThemeInputs is sufficient for Metaballs/Plasma without hardcoding demo palettes.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:31:56Z"},{"id":505,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.1.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Dependency Tweak (Plan Optimization)

This task was originally blocked by `bd-3vbf.1` (the full semantic design system).

That was overly constraining: we can define `ThemeInputs` *now* with a small set of semantically named slots (bg_base/bg_surface/bg_overlay/accents/etc.) and provide best-effort conversions from the **current** theme surfaces.

Then, when `bd-3vbf.1` lands, we can refine/expand the mapping without changing the FX public API. The stable boundary is `ThemeInputs`, not the upstream theme implementation.

Action:
- Changed `bd-l8x9.1.2 -> bd-3vbf.1` from `blocks` to `related`.

Rationale:
- Keeps FX extraction unblocked.
- Preserves the intention to align with the final design system.
- Avoids “compatibility shims”: the mapping can be corrected directly once the semantic palette exists.","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:55:54Z"}]}
